description: Embargo policy, then count of studies that have released data

turns.question: How long is the standard data embargo period on the portal?
turns.notes: Embargo policy is defined in documentation.

turns.question: How many studies have released data?
turns.notes: Live count of studies with released data requires querying the KG.

description: IRB requirements for human data, then access controls, then a real example

turns.question: What consent and IRB documentation is required when submitting human data?
turns.notes: Regulatory submission requirements are covered in the help docs.

turns.question: How do I control access to sensitive human data?
turns.notes: Access tier configuration and controlled-access process is documented in the help docs.

turns.question: Can you give an example of a controlled access dataset on the portal?
turns.notes: Retrieving a real example requires querying the KG for a controlled-access dataset.

description: Count of biobanks, then whether NF-OSI helps with sample requests

turns.question: How many biobanks are listed on the portal?
turns.notes: Current count requires querying the KG; docs won't have live numbers.

turns.question: Does NF-OSI help with requesting samples from a biobank?
turns.notes: Should sound like a doc question.

description: Accepted data types from docs, then which study has the most data types

turns.question: What data types are accepted by the portal?
turns.notes: Accepted data types are defined in the documentation.

turns.question: Which study has the most data types?
turns.notes: Ranking studies by number of distinct data types requires aggregating over the KG.

description: Out-of-scope analysis request pivots to docs on supported analysis platforms

turns.question: Can you help me write a script to analyze a portal dataset?
turns.notes: Direct analysis is out of scope; agent should decline and point to supported platforms.

turns.question: OK, what's the difference between cavatica, pluto.bio, cbioportal?
turns.notes: Comparison of supported analysis platforms is covered in the help docs.

turns.question: hi, is neurofibromatosis a cancer or autoimmune disease?
turns.notes: General medical background answerable from model knowledge; no portal KB needed.

description: Data contribution process for a new contributor

turns.question: What are the steps to contribute data to the NF Data Portal?
turns.notes: High-level contribution workflow; answer is entirely in help docs.

turns.question: What metadata fields are required when submitting files?
turns.notes: Metadata requirements are documented; cannot be answered by querying the graph.

description: Licensing policy from docs, then actual license distribution from graph

turns.question: What is the default license applied to non-human data submitted to the portal?
turns.notes: License policy is in the docs; no need to query the graph.

turns.question: What's the most common dataset license?
turns.notes: Requires aggregating license values across dataset entities in the KG; docs only describe the default policy, not actual distribution.

description: Exploring available NF1 cell line tools

turns.question: How many NF1 cell line tools are available in the portal?
turns.notes: Requires counting instances in the knowledge graph; docs won't have current counts.

turns.question: Show me NF1 cell lines derived from human donors
turns.notes: Filtering by donor type requires a SPARQL query over the KG.

turns.question: Which of those cell lines have loss-of-function NF1 mutations?
turns.notes: Follow-up filtering on mutation type; still a graph query. Uses conversation context.

turns.question: How many studies on the portal are focused on schwannomatosis?
turns.notes: Requires querying the graph for disease-specific study counts.

turns.question: What RNA-seq datasets are available for NF2 research?
turns.notes: Dataset listing by data type and disease; requires SPARQL over KG.

description: Contributor asks about embargo policy, then pivots to data inventory

turns.question: What is the data embargo policy for newly submitted datasets?
turns.notes: Policy/process question; clearly a documentation lookup.

turns.question: Take me to all the datasets that are currently publicly available
turns.notes: Navigation request; agent can redirect to filtered Datasets page directly without querying any KB.

description: Researcher explores data, then asks how to contribute

turns.question: How many datasets on the portal involve pediatric patients?
turns.notes: Requires querying the KG for patient age-related attributes.

turns.question: I'd like to add my pediatric NF1 study data to the portal. What do I need to prepare?
turns.notes: Pivot to contribution guidance; clearly a documentation topic despite prior graph context.

turns.question: What is the maximum file size for a single upload?
turns.notes: Technical upload constraint is in documentation.

description: Alternating docs and graph turns about NF model systems

turns.question: What types of NF model systems are supported by the portal?
turns.notes: Docs describe supported resource categories; graph can enumerate actual types present. Either source gives a valid answer.

turns.question: How many mouse models are there rn?
turns.notes: Current count requires querying the KG.

turns.question: How is info for these mouse models collected?
turns.notes: Should be in documentation.

turns.question: Can you walk me through downloading a real dataset on the portal, preferably one related to schwannomatosis?
turns.notes: Two-part question: identifying a real existing requires GRAPH; the download guide is in DOCS.

description: Ambiguous queries that could reasonably route to either source

turns.question: Tell me about NF1 research on the portal
turns.notes: Vague overview question; agent might use docs for context or graph for data inventory. BOTH is acceptable.

turns.question: Which clients are available for programmatic access?
turns.notes: Available clients (e.g. Synapse Python/R clients) are described in the docs.

description: Off-topic or general requests that should not trigger any KB lookup

turns.question: Help me write a Python script to reverse a string
turns.notes: General programming question unrelated to the NF portal; no KB lookup needed.

turns.question: OK, can you submit a support ticket on my behalf?
turns.notes: Requesting action assistant doesn't have

description: Funder asking for portfolio inventory counts and governance details

turns.question: How many NTAP-funded datasets are currently on the NF Data Portal?
turns.notes: Current inventory counts require querying the KG.

turns.question: I'm trying to get a complete picture of which funders have contributed datasets aside from the historical main funders
turns.notes: Intentionally open-ended. A well-routing agent should consult the docs for named major funders/initiatives and the KG to enumerate all funder entities directly on dataset entities. Neither source alone gives the complete picture.

turns.question: Which funder has the most initiatives?
turns.notes: Ranking funders by initiative count requires aggregating over the KG.

turns.question: Who should I contact to provide potential support for the portal?
turns.notes: Contact information for portal support/partnerships is in the docs; no KG lookup needed.
